info.name: ONHS UC2 — Camp Engagement
info.description:
## ONHS UC2 — Camp Engagement

**Scenario:** Swastha Karnataka Foundation (NGO) books a Swaasa AI cough analysis camp for 30 patients at Kanakapur Village Hall on 15 June 2026. Two payment paths and two edge-case variants are included.

**Protocol:** Beckn v2.0.0 + Health* schema pack v2.1
**Service:** Swaasa AI Cough Analysis (DIAGNOSTIC_ANALYTICS) | **Offer type:** CAMP | **Planned quantity:** 30 patients

### Quick-start: environment setup

1. Import **ONHS_UC2_Dev_Environment.json** into Postman (Environments → Import).
2. Select the **ONHS Dev** environment from the environment picker.
3. Set `bpp_uri`, `bap_uri`, and `auth_token` to your sandbox endpoints.
4. `network_id` is currently `onhs.in/health` (placeholder) — confirm with the network registry team.
5. UUID variable defaults are pre-filled; regenerate for separate test runs if needed.
6. **Scenario A/C:** set `entitlement_id` to the value issued at UC0 `on_confirm` (`f7a8b9c0-d1e2-3456-fa78-bc9012345ef6` pre-filled as default).

### Scenarios

**Scenario A — Entitlement Drawdown, 30 patients (60 steps: select → on_update)**
Swastha Karnataka Foundation draws down 30 units from the HealthEntitlement issued in UC0. No advance cash; `entitlementRef` in consideration signals the drawdown. Camp starts at `select` (no discover — seeker knows the provider from the entitlement record). End-of-camp update: 22 patients screened, 8 units credited back to entitlement.

**Scenario B — On-the-spot Payment, 30 patients (12 steps: discover → on_update)**
Camp organiser pays Rs.7500 upfront by NEFT — no prior entitlement. Full discover → on_discover lifecycle. `paymentAuthorisation` at confirm. End-of-camp: 22 patients screened; Rs.2000 on-actuals refund processed via `settlements[]`.

**Scenario C — Partial Completion (8 steps: select → on_update)**
Scenario A variant. Device failure halts camp after 15 patients. `update` carries partial headcount (15 of 30); `on_update` credits back 15 entitlement units and records `partialCompletionFraction=0.5`. Deletion attestation issued for unscreened patients (no artifact captured).

**Scenario D — Capacity Insufficient (2 steps: select → on_select)**
BAP commits 30 patients; entitlement only has 20 units remaining. BPP returns CANCELLED contract at `on_select` with `refusalReason=INSUFFICIENT_CAPACITY`.

### UC2-specific offer type

Offer type `CAMP` (per `HealthOffer.offerType` enum) distinguishes this engagement from `SINGLE_EVENT` (UC1) and `BULK_PROCUREMENT` (UC0). The CAMP offer carries `minimumQuantity`, `maximumQuantity`, and `requiresAdvanceBooking: true`.

### Bifurcation rule

A UC2 engagement carries exactly one of `{entitlementRef, paymentAuthorisation}` — never both.
- Scenarios A/C: consideration carries `entitlementRef` → no `paymentAuthorisation`
- Scenario B: consideration carries `paymentAuthorisation` → no `entitlementRef`

### HealthEntitlement campCommitment sub-object

At `on_confirm` (Scenarios A/C), `contractAttributes` carries the updated HealthEntitlement with a `campCommitment` sub-object (campId, campDate, campLocation, committedPatients, confirmedPatients, campStatus). campStatus lifecycle: ACTIVE (on_confirm) → ACTIVE (on_status mid-camp) → COMPLETED (on_update).

### Structural conventions (per core beckn.yaml v2.0.0)

- `select` carries a DRAFT `Contract` object (`SelectAction.contract` per core spec); BAP originates without `id`; BPP assigns `contract.id` at `on_select`
- `schemaContext` only on `discover`/`on_discover` (Scenario B); absent from all negotiation messages
- `OFFER_CAMP_REF` always includes `resourceIds: [\u007b\u007bresource_id\u007d\u007d]` (required per spec)
- `status` uses `message.contract {id, commitments[]}` — not a flat `contractId`
- `billingDetails`, `paymentInstructions`, `invoiceDetails` are banned from `Contract` (`additionalProperties: false`)
- Participant PII absent at `select`; full details from `init` onward
- `HealthContract.consent` present at `init`/`confirm` (BAP-initiated messages only) per IG §8.3; camp-batch consent recorded at `init` with `consentedBy: {{camp_coordinator_id}}`; BPP responses are exempt

### Consideration lifecycle

| Call | status.code | pricePerUnit | totalAmount | netAmount |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| catalog (on_discover, B only) | *(none)* | Rs.250 | — | — |
| select | DRAFT | — | — | — |
| on_select | PENDING | Rs.200 (A/C) / Rs.250 (B) | Rs.6000 / Rs.7500 | same |
| init | PENDING | echoed | echoed | echoed |
| on_init | QUOTED | Rs.200 / Rs.250 | Rs.6000 / Rs.7500 | same |
| confirm | ACTIVE | — | echoed | echoed |
| on_confirm → on_status | ACTIVE | — | planned total | planned total |
| on_update (full completion) | ACTIVE | — | 22×rate (A: Rs.4400, B: Rs.5500) | same |
| on_update (partial, C) | ACTIVE | — | 15×Rs.200 = Rs.3000 | same |

### End-of-camp settlement

| Scenario | Settlement key fields |
|---|---|
| A (full) | `entitlementRef`, `entitlementCreditBack: 8` |
| B (full) | `campLedgerRef`, `adjustmentType: DEBIT`, `adjustmentAmount: 2000` |
| C (partial) | `entitlementRef`, `entitlementCreditBack: 15` |

### Entitlement unit mechanics

**Entitlement unit = one patient analysis.** The entitlement pool (procured in UC0) is denominated in individual patient analyses — not in camps. One camp consuming 22 patients draws 22 units, not one "camp slot". The camp contract introduces a two-phase drawdown: the `select`→`on_confirm` cycle locks the planned quantity (30 units) against the entitlement's capacity at booking time, reducing `remainingCapacity` by 30; the `update`→`on_update` cycle reconciles actuals and releases unused units back, adjusting `usedCapacity` to the real headcount and restoring the unspent units to `remainingCapacity`. No money moves at either phase — the pool was pre-paid under UC0.

This keeps the entitlement pool fungible — the same UC0 pool serves any mix of individual UC1 engagements and UC2 camps without separate per-modality procurement. Locking at `select`→`on_confirm` also prevents race conditions when multiple camps draw concurrently: the BPP can reject a second camp's `on_select` if the combined committed quantity would exceed remaining capacity. And by establishing a ceiling at booking time, it gives the implementing agency an organisational boundary — no single camp can inadvertently consume the whole pool, which supports allocation planning across a programme.

### Cross-collection variable chaining

`entitlement_id` (`f7a8b9c0-d1e2-3456-fa78-bc9012345ef6`) is issued at UC0 `on_confirm` and consumed here in Scenarios A/C as the drawdown reference. Set this variable to the actual value returned by your UC0 run before executing Scenarios A/C.

item.name: Scenario A — Entitlement Drawdown (Batch Redemption)

request.description:
POST to CDS. Expect HTTP 200 ACK on acceptance.
HTTP 409 AckNoCallback = CDS accepted but will not send on_publish callback.
HTTP 400/401/500 = rejected — check error object in response body.
action enum: 'catalog/publish' (slash form). CDS also accepts 'catalog_publish' (underscore).

item.description: Swastha Karnataka Foundation draws down 30 units from the NGO HealthEntitlement (UC0). No advance cash payment; consideration carries entitlementRef. End-of-camp reconciliation: 22 patients screened, 8 units credited back. One unit = one patient analysis; the select cycle locks 30, the update cycle releases 8 back to the pool.

item.name: Scenario B — On-the-spot Payment

request.description:
POST to CDS. Expect HTTP 200 ACK on acceptance.
HTTP 409 AckNoCallback = CDS accepted but will not send on_publish callback.
HTTP 400/401/500 = rejected — check error object in response body.
action enum: 'catalog/publish' (slash form). CDS also accepts 'catalog_publish' (underscore).

item.description: Camp organiser pays Rs.7500 upfront (NEFT). Full discover→on_update lifecycle. End-of-camp: 22 patients screened, Rs.2000 on-actuals refund to be processed.

item.name: Scenario C — Partial Completion (Device Failure)

request.description:
POST to CDS. Expect HTTP 200 ACK on acceptance.
HTTP 409 AckNoCallback = CDS accepted but will not send on_publish callback.
HTTP 400/401/500 = rejected — check error object in response body.
action enum: 'catalog/publish' (slash form). CDS also accepts 'catalog_publish' (underscore).

item.description: Scenario A (entitlement drawdown) variant. Device failure halts camp after 15 patients. update carries partial headcount; on_update credits back 15 units. One unit = one patient analysis; 30 locked at select, 15 released at update (partialCompletionFraction=0.5).

item.name: Scenario D — Capacity Insufficient (Failure Path)

request.description:
POST to CDS. Expect HTTP 200 ACK on acceptance.
HTTP 409 AckNoCallback = CDS accepted but will not send on_publish callback.
HTTP 400/401/500 = rejected — check error object in response body.
action enum: 'catalog/publish' (slash form). CDS also accepts 'catalog_publish' (underscore).

item.description: BAP attempts to commit 30 patients; entitlement only has 20 units remaining. BPP returns CANCELLED contract at on_select with refusalReason=INSUFFICIENT_CAPACITY.

variable.description: CDS (Catalog Discovery Service) base URL — confirm with ONHS network team
Scenario A contract UUID (BPP assigns at on_select)
Scenario B contract UUID (BPP assigns at on_select)
CAMP offer — offerType: CAMP, used in Scenarios A/B/C/D
IMPLEMENTING_AGENCY — Swastha Karnataka Foundation (same as UC0/UC1)
Unique camp identifier — used in contractAttributes.campId and campCommitment.campId
Issued at UC0 on_confirm. Set to the value returned by your UC0 run before executing Scenarios A/C/D.
Scenario B — NEFT payment UTR from camp organiser
Scenario B — seeker-side cash collection ledger reference
SHA256 checksum of NGO-Darpan certificate — set before running Scenario A/B/C/D select
